title: Cosmos Vortex — mic-reactive spiral whirlpool visualizer
summary: A swirling spiral-vortex visualizer: each arm's point brightness and length ride the spectrum while the spin speed rides the overall level, tightening on beats into a glowing whirlpool. Reacts to the MICROPHONE live (gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable/denied) plus a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero-deps.
usage_notes: RUN: open and click "enable mic" to make the whirlpool swirl to your voice/music; it falls back to a built-in beat if no mic is granted. KEY TECHNIQUES: an AnalyserNode feeds a 64-bin spectrum + level into render(spectrum, level, t); each spiral arm samples the spectrum along its length for brightness/length, the level sets spin speed, and a low-band beat detector tightens the swirl; a seeded synthSpectrum(u) drives the same render for auto/fallback. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: ARMS and PTS (spiral density), the tight factor (swirl tightness), LOOP (cycle length), and the beat threshold in detectBeat.
